messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Generative AI for Statistical Science (Free Seminar) – last chance
messages.subject_clean: Generative AI for Statistical Science (Free Seminar) – last chance
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Last chance to join our 1-day seminar Generative AI for Statistical Science (Free Seminar) livestreaming March 3 with Lei Ding (Department of Statistics, University of Manitoba). This seminar introduces modern generative AI paradigms, covering models like VAEs, GANs, and diffusion models, which are powerful tools for approximating high-dimensional probability distributions. Researchers in genetic data analysis can leverage these methods for tasks such as generating synthetic genetic datasets for privacy-preserving research or for advanced imputation of missing genetic markers. Understanding these statistical foundations will enable the R-sig-genetics community to innovate in modeling complex genetic architectures and address critical issues like data privacy and bias in their studies.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/generative-ai-for-statistical-science-fr) to secure your spot, and please share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Useful Tools for Data Science in R – online seminar
messages.subject_clean: Useful Tools for Data Science in R – online seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Useful Tools for Data Science in R led by SMCS (Universit? Catholique de Louvain) is now available as an on-demand streaming seminar. This seminar explores powerful tools like R, GitHub, Copilot, and Visual Studio Code to enhance data science workflows. Researchers analyzing genetic data can leverage these skills to streamline their R scripting, manage complex analysis pipelines with robust version control, and collaborate more effectively on large-scale genomic projects. Integrating AI-powered coding assistance and efficient development environments will accelerate the development of novel genetic models and ensure the reproducibility and integrity of their research findings.

Access this seminar (https://instats.org/seminar/useful-tools-for-data-science-in-r-1) at your own pace and please share this with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Advanced R Spatial Analysis
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Discover advanced methods for analyzing spatial data effectively in your work. Advanced R for Spatial Analysis led by Nick Bearman (University College London and Geospatial Training Solutions) is now available as an on-demand streaming seminar. This seminar focuses on advanced spatial analysis techniques in R, including spatial joins, buffering, and point-in-polygon operations. Researchers in genetics can apply these methods to analyze geographically distributed genetic data, understand population structures in relation to environmental factors, or map disease prevalence. Mastering these skills will enable them to integrate diverse spatial datasets, enhancing the precision and scope of their genetic research and contributing to more robust findings.

Access this seminar (https://instats.org/seminar/advanced-r-for-spatial-analysis-1) whenever convenient and feel free to circulate this with colleagues and students who may be interested!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Last Chance: AI for Systematic Reviews
messages.subject_clean: Last Chance: AI for Systematic Reviews
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Last chance - This seminar offers practical, hands-on training to enhance your research workflow and evidence synthesis capabilities. AI for Systematic Reviews is a 2-day seminar livestreaming March 11?12 with Hadi Fariborzi (Bissett School of Business, Mount Royal University, Calgary). This seminar introduces a rigorous AI-integrated workflow for systematic reviews, demonstrating how to manage the accelerating volume of genetic literature efficiently. Researchers in genetic data analysis can apply these methods to build reproducible searches, conduct AI-assisted screening, and create analysis-ready datasets for their own meta-analyses. The course emphasizes quantitative workflows, including advanced meta-analysis techniques demonstrated in R, which can directly enhance the rigor and scope of genetic research.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/ai-for-systematic-reviews) to join before enrollment closes, and please pass this to colleagues and students who may be interested!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Marine Genomics course - online, 25-29 May
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We are excited to announce that our Marine Genomics course, originally planned in-person, will now be fully online to foster international participation!
 
Over five days (25?29 May), this course offers hands-on training in:

Genome assembly assessment and variant calling

Population genomics analyses with marine datasets

Integrating genomic and environmental data for seascape genomics

Interpreting results for ecology, conservation, and fisheries
The course combines lectures, discussions, and practical exercises using real marine datasets. It is designed for graduate students, postdocs, and researchers working in marine and population genomics. 
 
You can find more information about the course here: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/marine-genomics/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/marine-genomics/ )
 
 
 
Best regards, Carlo

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Evidence Synthesis and Meta-analysis in R | Online Course, 11–14 May
messages.subject_clean: Evidence Synthesis and Meta-analysis in R | Online Course, 11–14 May
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,

 
You can still join our 4-day online course ?Meta-analysis in R? (11?14 May) to learn how to conduct rigorous and reproducible evidence synthesis in ecology and evolution.
 
In this hands-on course, participants will learn how to design and perform systematic reviews and meta-analyses, from question formulation and literature searching to effect size calculation, meta-analytic modelling, and interpretation of results. The course also covers multilevel meta-analysis, meta-regression, heterogeneity, publication bias, and data visualization, using R and widely used packages such as metafor and orchaRd.
 
The program combines lectures and practical exercises with real datasets, providing participants with the skills needed to apply evidence synthesis methods to their own research projects.
 
If you are interested in learning how to integrate findings across studies and perform robust meta-analyses in R, this course will provide the practical tools to get started: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/metain-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/metain-r/ ) 
 
Best regards,
 
Carlo
 
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Last chance: Causal Inference using AI and ML
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Causal Inference using AI and ML
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Last chance - This upcoming seminar offers an intensive exploration of critical methods for robust research. Causal Inference with AI and Machine Learning is a 2-day seminar livestreaming March 12?13 with Jason Anastasopoulos (School of Public and International Affairs, University of Georgia). This seminar explores the logic and practice of causal inference, moving beyond mere prediction to understand cause-and-effect relationships. Researchers in genetic data analysis can apply these methods to identify causal genetic variants, understand gene-environment interactions, or evaluate the impact of genetic interventions. Participants will learn to design credible studies, implement state-of-the-art estimators in R, and leverage LLM-assisted prompting for robust diagnostics and clear explanations in their own genetic research.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/causal-inference-with-ai-and-machine-lea) to join before enrollment closes, and please pass this to colleagues and students who may be interested!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Last chance: R Programming for Statistics and ML
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: R Programming for Statistics and ML
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Last chance - This seminar offers practical R programming skills to advance your statistical and machine learning work. R Programming for Statistics and Machine Learning is a 2-day seminar livestreaming March 27 with Nikolay Oskolkov (Group Leader (PI) at LIOS). This seminar explores R programming as an integrated toolkit for contemporary statistical inference and machine learning, emphasizing both theoretical foundations and practical implementation for reproducible research. Researchers analyzing genetic data can apply these methods, including penalized regression, advanced dimensionality reduction, and sophisticated clustering, to identify key genetic markers, classify complex traits, and build robust predictive models. Learning to implement these algorithms from first principles in R will enhance capabilities for methodological innovation and ensure transparent, publication-ready analyses of high-dimensional genetic datasets.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/r-programming-for-statistics-and-machine) to register now, and we'd appreciate you sharing this with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Last chance: RNA-seq: FASTQ to Counts with Nextflow
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: RNA-seq: FASTQ to Counts with Nextflow
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Last chance - Learn to establish robust, reproducible RNA-seq processing pipelines for your genetic research. RNA-seq: From FASTQ to Counts is a 2-day seminar livestreaming March 23 and 25 with Estefania Mancini (Josep Carreras Leukaemia Research Institute). This seminar offers practical, hands-on training in reproducible RNA-seq data processing using nf-core Nextflow pipelines. Researchers can apply these skills to generate robust, publication-ready read count matrices from raw FASTQ files, ensuring their upstream data preparation is sound before statistical analysis in R. You will gain the ability to configure and run portable, containerized workflows on various computing environments, troubleshoot common issues, and document workflows for enhanced reproducibility in your genetic data analysis projects.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/rna-seq-from-fastq-to-counts) to reserve your place before it starts, and feel free to circulate this with colleagues and students!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Computational Genomics for Microbial Trait Analysis
messages.subject_clean: Computational Genomics for Microbial Trait Analysis
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Explore advanced computational genomics techniques to understand microbial adaptation and phenotypic outcomes. Computational Genomics for Microbial Traits (Free Seminar) is a 2-day seminar livestreaming April 22, led by Janani Ravi (University of Colorado Anschutz). This seminar explores general-purpose computational approaches that integrate large-scale, heterogeneous public datasets to bridge the microbial genotype-phenotype gap. It demonstrates how machine learning and comparative genomics can study microbial adaptation and predict antimicrobial resistance, recovering known mechanisms and discovering novel candidates. Researchers in genetic data analysis can apply these microbe- and disease-agnostic methods, open-source software, and interactive web tools to advance their own work on microbial traits, host responses, and drug repurposing.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/computational-genomics-for-microbial-tra) to join the livestream, and please pass this along to colleagues and students who may find it useful!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Spring School in Bioinformatics (9th edition)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We are pleased to announce our upcoming Spring School in Bioinformatics (9th edition), which will take place online from 8?12 June.
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course68/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course68/ )
 
 
This five-day course will introduce participants to the fundamentals of Next Generation Sequencing (NGS) data analysis, combining conceptual lectures with extensive hands-on practical sessions. Participants will learn how to assess sequencing quality, assemble and annotate genomes, perform RNA-seq and differential gene expression analyses, and explore phylogenomics using NGS data. While the course will primarily focus on Illumina data, it will also cover the opportunities and requirements of long-read technologies such as Nanopore and PacBio.
 
Throughout the week, participants will also gain practical experience with the Linux command line, as well as with Docker and software containers, which are widely used in bioinformatics to ensure reproducible analyses.
 
The course is designed for researchers with a biological background who would like to gain practical experience in handling and analysing genomic and transcriptomic data. By the end of the training, participants will have a solid understanding of the strengths and limitations of NGS approaches and the tools commonly used in modern bioinformatics workflows.
 
For the full list of our courses and workshops, please visit: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course68/ )
 
 
Best regards,
Carlo

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] UMAP & Dimensionality Reduction in R
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Learn to simplify complex genetic datasets and uncover new insights using advanced analytical techniques. Dimensionality Reduction with UMAP and Beyond 2.0 led by Nikolay Oskolkov (Group Leader (PI) at LIOS) is now available as an on-demand streaming seminar. This seminar offers a hands-on approach to essential dimensionality reduction techniques, crucial for anyone in genetic data analysis working with large multivariate datasets. By simplifying complex genetic datasets, these methods enable researchers to discover new insights and make data manageable for further analysis, improving accuracy and efficiency. Participants will gain practical experience in applying methods like UMAP, tSNE, and PCA using R and Python, directly enhancing their analytical skills for ongoing and future genetic projects.

Access this seminar (https://instats.org/seminar/dimensionality-reduction-with-umap-and-b-1) whenever convenient and feel free to circulate this with colleagues and students who may be interested!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
